nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Goronwy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Gronow
meanings: Welsh: relationship name from the Welsh personal name Goronwy (also found as Gronw and Gronow, from Old Welsh Guorgonui).

nameMeanings.name: Meredydd
meanings: This is said to be the same name as Meriadek or Meiradwg, and also as the] Irish name O'Muireadwg, and the Scotch Mac Muireadaigh and Muir- eadhach. O'Reilly, however, renders the Irish word muireadach 'a sovereign, a lord j also a man's name, Morogh.' It means rather l admiral,' literally protector of the sea; from muir the sea, eadhach protector. I derive the British name from mawreddwg, mawreddig, magnificent, great, from mawr, great.

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Tudur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from the Old Celtic form Teutorix, composed of elements meaning ‘people, tribe’ and ‘ruler, king’. It has been described as a Welsh form of Theodore, but there is no connection between them, though it is ultimately akin to the Germanic name Theodoric ( see Terry ).

variants.name: Tudor
meanings: The Anglicised form of the Welsh Tewdwr, a name derived from Theodore.

variants.name: Tudyr
meanings: Traditional: from the Old Celtic form Teutorix, composed of elements meaning ‘people, tribe’ and ‘ruler, king’. It has been described as a Welsh form of Theodore, but there is no connection between them, though it is ultimately akin to the Germanic name Theodoric ( see Terry ).

en.lifeSummary: Meredydd Ap Tudur Ap Goronwy was born about 1144, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter.
